titleStmt.title: Letter from Henry Arthur Wynne to Hugh Magrane, 22 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Henry Arthur Wynne

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Henry Arthur Wynne (1867-1943), the chief crown solicitor for Ireland from 1916 to 1922, to Hugh Magrane. The letter regards the a previous letter sent by Magrane regarding the death of his cousin Alice Martin (1844-1916), in which he requested governmental help in claiming Martin's insurance. In this letter, Wynne denies the request.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5611/8481/2

correspAction.placeName: 36 Mill Street, Newry, County Down, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
22nd May 1916
re Mrs Alice Martin, 36 Mill St., Newry. Sir, With reference to your letter of 15th instant the Attorney
General has intimated that the crown will not interfere in
this matter. You can act therefore as you may be advised. I am,
Sir
Your obedient servant, Henry Arthur Wynne Dr Hugh Magrane,
35 Mill Street,
Newry.
